chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so just like we have our aggregation functions, we also have our power functions

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: and if we want to calculate a to the power b, then we have this function

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: let's use it here

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: let's suppose we want to calculate the square for every element

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so np.square is the function that we can use to calculate the square for all of the values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: similarly, we can use square root function to calculate the square root of all the values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: and we can also have the power function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: in power function, if we want to calculate every array to the power of 3

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we have these values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: 1 to the power 3 is 3, 2 to the power 3 is 8, 3 to the power 3 is 27

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: 4 to the power 3 is 64 and so on

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so these are our power functions

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like we have power functions, we also have log related functions

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we calculate logarithmic values in log

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we must have studied about log in school

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we want to calculate log

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: by the way, we should not know how to calculate in-depth log

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: we should know that this function exists whenever we want to calculate log value

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: let's have the same array

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: first of all, function is to calculate the natural log

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we want to calculate the natural log of all the values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we can use log function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we will get all the natural logs

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we want to change the base

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so the base in natural log is e

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: e is a special symbol in math

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so base is e in natural log

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: but let's suppose if we want to change the base

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: we want to calculate log base 10

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: we want to calculate log base 2

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we can do that too

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we do log 10, then log base 10 will be calculated

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: similarly if we do log 2, then log base 2 will be calculated

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: 4 is in the power of 2

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so log of 4 base 2 is equal to value 2

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we will get 2

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: log of 2 base 2 is equal to value 1

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we will get 1

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like log is function to calculate

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: similarly, exponent is also function to calculate

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we will get all the exponential values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so whenever we want to calculate e to the power x

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then we can use this function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: we also have functions for rounding values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we want to round off values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then we have special functions for that too

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like let's suppose if

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: i want to round off value 3.14

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so if we want to round off 3.14 from nearest value

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like 3.1 is nearest to 3 or 4?

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: 3 is there, so we will get value of 3 equal

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we had 9.4 here, then value would have been rounded off to 4

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if let's suppose we want to round up

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then in that case we use the seal function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: round up means whatever is the upper value, round off will be there

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like seal is short for ceiling

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: which is the roof

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: opposite of this we have floor function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so whenever we want to round down

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then in that case we use floor function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we want, we can truncate our decimal values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: for that we have function trunc

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: truncate means to cut things

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so if we want to print values after decimal

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then we can use truncate function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so these are the functions for rounding off

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: apart from this we have many other functions

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like we also have a unique function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: a function called unique

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so we have np.unique

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: in which if we pass an array

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then we will get all unique values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so here 1,2,3,4,5 are all unique values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: but if i repeat some values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: like 5,8 and run

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then we will get all unique values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: it's basically kind of like how a set works

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: apart from this we also have

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: sort function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: which sorts all our values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: we also have abs function

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: abs function is for absolute values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: what does abs function do?

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: if we have positive values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: in fact let's take it in this array

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: some values are positive

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: some values are negative

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so abs function will sort all values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so if all values are negative

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: then abs function will keep positive values positive

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: and make negative values positive

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so it will give us all absolute values

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: without the sign

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: so in this way

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: all our values will be positive

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: or we will get absolute value

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: in return

chunks.title: Other
chunks.text: and not only this

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other

chunks.title: Other
